prevalence.source: IHME GBD / Brazilian psychiatric epidemiology syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable; diagnosis improving with mental health service expansion

burden.mortalityRate: Indirect; elevated suicide and comorbidity risk

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11: history of manic/hypomanic episodes (with or without major depression); differentiation from unipolar depression is critical

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: low; often diagnosed after severe episodes
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; relapse common without maintenance and psychosocial support

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Specialist diagnosis more common; APS may miss hypomania history
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Mood stabilizers (lithium, valproate) and atypical antipsychotics; psychoeducation; crisis care in CAPS for severe episodes
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Misdiagnosis as unipolar depression, limited psychotherapy/psychoeducation access, and adherence barriers
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: CAPS network for severe mental illness and access to core mood stabilizers/antipsychotics
